<h1> StegoRSA </h1>

<img width="1371" height="412" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5c23f91c-7b5e-4efa-b127-6dfc2e9a2a86" />

---


## Start Lab

### Challenge Overview
This challenge combines Steganography (hiding data) and RSA Cryptography. The creator encrypted a flag using RSA but accidentally hid the private key inside the image's metadata as a hexadecimal string. Your goal is to extract that hex string, convert it back into a usable private key, and decrypt the message.

````
mkdir StegoRSA
cd StegoRSA
````
````
wget https://challenge-files.picoctf.net/c_plain_mesa/857696a027f6c65f47cf7b8c910430903e7db36e8c9ee183277ad76fa11292dc/flag.enc
````
<img width="1810" height="446" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b068088a-86e9-49e7-882b-2b009024b08b" />


````
wget https://challenge-files.picoctf.net/c_plain_mesa/857696a027f6c65f47cf7b8c910430903e7db36e8c9ee183277ad76fa11292dc/image.jpg
````
<img width="1867" height="304" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4ebd06c6-ecf5-4e70-96fc-a05913ec4053" />


````
ls
````
<img width="508" height="152" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dd6ccca9-86a9-4e11-bca8-a2088a2babb4" />


- Tools that we can use:
````
file
strings
exiftool
binwalk
steghide
stegseek
````
---




### Inspect the Image Metadata

The first hint says: "Metadata can tell you more than you expect." We need to look at the hidden data inside the image file (usually named ``stego.png`` or similar in the downloaded zip).

- Install `Exiftool`:
````
sudo apt install libimage-exiftool-perl -y
````
<img width="1296" height="930" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/82ad7d04-26f8-4d2f-b3d1-2c44e5a8a1c3" />


````
exiftool image.jpg
````
<img width="1899" height="856" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7ce35368-881e-4d9f-81ef-b24d268ce441" />

Now we see it very long hex string.

Scroll through the output. We will see a field (often Comment, Description, or a custom tag) containing a very long string of hexadecimal characters (numbers 0-9 and letters A-F).


In this we can use with `Cyberchef`, but i want to use command.

### Convert the Hex String to a Key File
The second hint says: "Hex can be turned back into a key file." We need to convert that text representation of hex back into binary data to create a private key file.

#### Create a text file containing the hex string:
````
echo "" > hex_key.txt
````
<img width="1906" height="475" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/71171c53-b43f-401d-ad4e-b2efdc6a5bce" />


#### Use **xxd** to reverse the hex into a binary file (usually a DER formatted key):
````
sudo apt install xxd -y
````
<img width="1357" height="1035" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3671cdc6-2bc3-4e38-ae29-8f3470351499" />

````
xxd -r -p hex_key.txt > private_key.der
````
<img width="730" height="148" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/959eb2ed-d0a6-4b02-b4e9-d838cdfb19ec" />

```
cat private_key.der
```
<img width="813" height="649" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d78463c6-b3d2-4ecc-b8a1-d2b7fa75d001" />


````
mv private_key.der private_key.pem
ls
openssl pkeyutl -decrypt -in flag.enc -inkey private_key.pem -out flag.txt
ls
cat flag.txt
````
<img width="942" height="380" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/11751b15-bd9e-480f-a7f6-6b57d0d9712d" />
Now we got the correct flag.

---



### Flag:
````
picoCTF{rs4_k3y_1n_1mg_26586619}
````
<img width="1381" height="446" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ef2c8a43-083b-4556-bf7c-4eb4de055889" />

---
